Home › Blog
From Our Physicians

Health & Wellness Blog

Evidence-based articles from Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman — on Direct Primary Care, Lifestyle Medicine, nutrition, sleep, chronic disease, and living well on the Main Line.

concierge medicine Main Line PAconcierge doctor Wayne PA

Concierge Medicine on the Main Line: What Philadelphia Suburbs Need to Know

Looking for a concierge doctor in Wayne, Radnor, or Villanova? Here's what Main Line PA patients should know about DPC and concierge medicine options.

Forward Family Medicine February 10, 2026
DPC employer benefitsdirect primary care for employers

Why Employers Are Turning to Direct Primary Care as a Benefits Strategy

DPC membership combined with a high-deductible plan can cut employer health costs while giving employees better primary care access. Here's how it works.

Forward Family Medicine February 3, 2026
how to read EOBexplanation of benefits explained

How to Read an Explanation of Benefits (EOB): What You're Actually Being Charged

The EOB is not a bill — but it matters. Learn to read every column, spot billing errors, understand balance billing, and see why DPC eliminates EOBs for primary care.

Forward Family Medicine January 27, 2026
health insurance basicsunderstanding health insurance

Health Insurance Basics Every Patient Should Understand

Deductibles, copays, coinsurance, prior auth — decoded. Plus how the DPC and HDHP combination changes what these terms mean for your actual costs.

Forward Family Medicine January 20, 2026
what is direct primary caredirect primary care explained

What Is Direct Primary Care? A Complete Patient Guide

Direct primary care is a membership-based model where you pay a flat monthly fee for unlimited primary care — no insurance billing, no copays, just your doctor.

Forward Family Medicine January 14, 2026
why patients leave doctorschanging primary care doctor

Why Patients Leave Their Primary Care Doctors (And What to Look For Instead)

Rushed visits, long waits, billing problems — patients switch doctors for real reasons. Learn what drives people to leave and what to look for in your next physician.

Forward Family Medicine January 14, 2026
women's preventive healthwomen's health screenings

Women's Preventive Health: Your Complete Screening Guide by Decade

A decade-by-decade guide to women's preventive screenings — from Pap smears in your 20s to bone density and menopause discussions in your 50s and beyond.

Forward Family Medicine January 14, 2026
HSA direct primary carecan you use HSA for DPC

Can You Use an HSA with Direct Primary Care? What You Need to Know

DPC membership fees are not currently HSA-eligible, but labs, medications, and procedures often are. Here's the full picture on HSA and DPC — and the strategy that works.

Forward Family Medicine January 13, 2026
DPC specialist referralshow DPC referrals work

How Specialist Referrals Work in Direct Primary Care

DPC covers primary care, not specialist visits — but your DPC physician plays a central role in coordinating, advocating, and following up on specialist care.

Forward Family Medicine January 6, 2026
telehealth primary carevirtual doctor visit

Telehealth and Primary Care: When Virtual Visits Work (and When They Don't)

Telehealth works well for follow-ups, medication refills, and minor illness — but not everything. Here's an honest guide to when virtual visits make sense.

Forward Family Medicine December 21, 2025
DPC telehealthdirect primary care virtual visits

Telehealth in Direct Primary Care: How Virtual Visits Work

DPC telehealth isn't a stranger on a video call — it's your own physician who knows your history, included in your membership with no per-visit fee. Here's how it works.

Forward Family Medicine December 16, 2025
how to avoid ER visitavoiding emergency room

How to Avoid Unnecessary Emergency Room Visits (Without Ignoring Real Emergencies)

Learn which conditions truly require the ER, when urgent care or your primary care doctor is the right call, and how same-day DPC access changes the math.

Forward Family Medicine December 9, 2025
DPC vs urgent caredirect primary care urgent care

DPC vs. Urgent Care: When to Use Each (and How DPC Replaces Most Urgent Care Visits)

Urgent care costs $150–$300 per visit and often handles conditions your DPC doctor can treat same-day. Here's when DPC replaces urgent care — and when it doesn't.

Forward Family Medicine December 2, 2025
primary care busy professionalswhy busy people skip doctor

Primary Care for Busy Professionals: Why You Keep Skipping the Doctor (and How to Fix It)

Busy professionals skip primary care because the system doesn't work for them. Here's why that's a costly mistake and how DPC changes the equation.

Forward Family Medicine November 25, 2025
concierge medicine busy professionalsconcierge doctor executives

Concierge Medicine for Busy Professionals: Is It Right for You?

Busy professionals need same-day access, telehealth, and a doctor who knows them. Here's why concierge and DPC medicine is designed for exactly that.

Forward Family Medicine November 18, 2025
direct primary care self-employedDPC for freelancers

Direct Primary Care for the Self-Employed: The Healthcare Solution You've Been Missing

Self-employed? DPC paired with a high-deductible plan gives you excellent primary care, lower overall costs, and no dependence on an employer for healthcare access.

Forward Family Medicine November 11, 2025
direct primary care for familiesfamily DPC membership

Direct Primary Care for Families: Is It Worth It?

DPC membership for families means one doctor who knows everyone, no per-visit fees for sick kids, and real savings over copays and deductibles. Here's how it works.

Forward Family Medicine November 4, 2025
doctor meet and greetwhat is a meet and greet doctor

What Is a Doctor Meet and Greet? What to Ask Before You Enroll

Many DPC practices offer a free meet-and-greet before you enroll. Here's what happens, the questions that matter most, and how to know if it's the right fit.

Forward Family Medicine October 28, 2025
how to prepare for doctor appointmentwhat to bring to doctor

How to Prepare for Your Doctor Appointment to Get the Most Out of It

Walk into your next appointment with the right information, the right questions, and a clear plan — and walk out with answers instead of regrets.

Forward Family Medicine October 21, 2025
second medical opinionwhen to get second opinion doctor

When and How to Get a Second Medical Opinion

Know when a second opinion is essential, how to ask for one without awkwardness, and what to bring — plus how a DPC doctor makes the process easier.

Forward Family Medicine October 14, 2025
managing multiple medicationspolypharmacy risks

How to Manage Multiple Medications Safely: A Guide for Patients

Polypharmacy affects millions of Americans. Learn how to maintain an accurate medication list, reduce interaction risks, and work with your doctor on deprescribing.

Forward Family Medicine October 7, 2025
how to read lab resultsunderstanding blood test results

How to Read Your Lab Results: A Patient's Plain-English Guide

A plain-English guide to the most common blood tests — CBC, metabolic panel, lipid panel, HbA1c, and TSH — so you can understand what your results actually mean.

Forward Family Medicine September 30, 2025
how to find a good primary care doctorfinding a primary care physician

How to Find a Primary Care Doctor You Actually Like

Finding a good primary care doctor takes more than a zip code search. Here's a practical guide to what actually matters and how to evaluate your options.

Forward Family Medicine September 23, 2025
primary care over 50health priorities 50s

Primary Care After 50: What Shifts and What to Prioritize

After 50, screenings expand, chronic disease risk rises, and the value of continuity with a physician who knows you grows significantly. Here's what to focus on.

Forward Family Medicine September 16, 2025
direct primary care chronic diseaseDPC for diabetes

Managing Chronic Conditions with Direct Primary Care: A Better Approach

Chronic conditions like diabetes and hypertension need regular attention, not 10-minute visits. DPC's longer appointments and direct access make real disease management possible.

Forward Family Medicine September 9, 2025
chronic pain primary caremanaging chronic pain

Chronic Pain and Primary Care: A Whole-Person Approach

Chronic pain affects 1 in 5 adults. Learn how lifestyle medicine, multimodal treatment, and a DPC physician's longitudinal care can address the root drivers — not just the symptoms.

Forward Family Medicine September 2, 2025
gut health primary caregut microbiome doctor

Gut Health: What Primary Care Physicians Actually Know (and Can Help With)

Separating gut health science from wellness industry hype. Learn what primary care can diagnose and treat — and when to be skeptical of popular claims.

Forward Family Medicine August 26, 2025
men's health screeningspreventive care for men

Men's Preventive Health: The Screenings and Habits That Matter Most

Men are 50% less likely to have a regular primary care doctor than women. Here are the screenings and habits that make the biggest difference — and why they're worth prioritizing.

Forward Family Medicine August 19, 2025
health screenings 40spreventive screenings age 40

Preventive Health Screenings You Need in Your 40s

The 40s are the most important decade to lock in preventive habits. Here are the screenings recommended by USPSTF and ACS — and why they matter now.

Forward Family Medicine August 12, 2025
colorectal cancer screeningcolon cancer screening options

Colorectal Cancer Screening: Your Options, Explained

The ACS now recommends colorectal cancer screening starting at 45. Compare colonoscopy, Cologuard, FIT, and CT colonography to find the right option for you.

Forward Family Medicine August 5, 2025
social connection and healthloneliness health effects

Why Social Connection Is a Medical Issue (Not Just a Nice-to-Have)

Social isolation increases mortality risk equivalent to smoking 15 cigarettes per day. The US Surgeon General and ACLM recognize connection as a health pillar.

Forward Family Medicine July 29, 2025
home blood pressure monitoringhow to check blood pressure at home

Home Blood Pressure Monitoring: How to Do It Right and When It Matters

Home blood pressure monitoring gives a more accurate picture of your true BP than office readings alone. Here is how to do it correctly and what the numbers mean.

Forward Family Medicine July 22, 2025
when to check cholesterolcholesterol screening age

When Should You Start Getting Your Cholesterol Checked?

ACC/AHA guidelines recommend a lipid panel starting at age 20. Learn what your numbers mean, how often to test, and when medication becomes the right choice.

Forward Family Medicine July 15, 2025
lifestyle medicine hypertensionlower blood pressure naturally

Managing High Blood Pressure with Lifestyle: What Actually Works

Stage 1 hypertension is often treatable with lifestyle alone. See the evidence on DASH diet, sodium, exercise, and weight loss — with actual numbers.

Forward Family Medicine July 8, 2025
alcohol and healthis alcohol bad for you

What the Latest Research Says About Alcohol and Your Health

The WHO now states there is no safe level of alcohol. Mendelian randomization studies debunk the J-curve. Here's what the updated evidence actually shows.

Forward Family Medicine July 1, 2025
DPC mental healthprimary care mental health

DPC and Mental Health: How Primary Care Fills the Behavioral Health Gap

With psychiatric wait times stretching months, primary care manages most mental health in the US. DPC's structure makes that care meaningfully better.

Forward Family Medicine June 24, 2025
mental health primary caredepression primary care

Mental Health and Primary Care: Why Your Doctor Should Be Part of Your Mental Health Team

Most mental health care in the US happens in primary care offices. Learn what your doctor can manage, when referral is right, and how DPC makes mental health care better.

Forward Family Medicine June 17, 2025
how stress causes diseasechronic stress health effects

How Chronic Stress Makes You Sick: The Physiology Explained

Chronic stress activates the HPA axis, raising cortisol in ways that drive inflammation, insulin resistance, and cardiovascular disease. Here's the science.

Forward Family Medicine June 10, 2025
sleep and weight gaindoes poor sleep cause weight gain

The Sleep-Weight Connection: How Poor Sleep Makes You Gain Weight

Poor sleep raises hunger hormones, lowers insulin sensitivity, and increases calorie intake. Here's what the research shows about sleep and weight management.

Forward Family Medicine June 3, 2025
weight management primary careobesity primary care treatment

Weight Management and Primary Care: What Your Doctor Can Actually Do

Primary care can do far more than 'eat less, move more.' Learn about the real tools available for weight management, from GLP-1 medications to lifestyle medicine.

Forward Family Medicine May 27, 2025
longevity medicinepreventive longevity care

Longevity Medicine: What It Is and How Primary Care Fits In

Longevity medicine focuses on extending healthy, functional years — not just lifespan. Primary care, done well, is the foundation of this approach.

Forward Family Medicine May 20, 2025
strength training after 40weight training 40s benefits

Why Everyone Over 40 Should Be Strength Training

Sarcopenia starts in your 30s and accelerates after 40. Resistance training is the intervention — not for aesthetics, but for metabolic health, bone density, and longevity.

Forward Family Medicine May 13, 2025
exercise as medicinephysical activity health benefits

Exercise as Medicine: Why Physical Activity Is the Most Evidence-Supported Health Intervention

Exercise prevents and treats 26 chronic diseases, rivals antidepressants for depression, and extends lifespan. Here's what the evidence actually shows.

Forward Family Medicine May 6, 2025
diabetes prevention programCDC diabetes prevention

The Diabetes Prevention Program: What It Is and Who Should Do It

The CDC-recognized Diabetes Prevention Program reduces type 2 diabetes risk by 58%. Learn who qualifies, what the program involves, and how DPC supports it.

Forward Family Medicine April 29, 2025
lifestyle medicine diabetestype 2 diabetes lifestyle treatment

Lifestyle Medicine for Type 2 Diabetes: What the Evidence Shows

Evidence shows intensive lifestyle changes can achieve T2DM remission and reduce medication burden. Learn how the DPP and DiRECT trials changed what we know.

Forward Family Medicine April 22, 2025
mindful eatingmindful eating health benefits

Mindful Eating: The Evidence Behind a Surprisingly Powerful Health Practice

Mindful eating reduces caloric intake, cuts binge eating episodes, and improves food satisfaction — without restriction. Here's what the research actually shows.

Forward Family Medicine April 15, 2025
anti-inflammatory foodsfoods that reduce inflammation

Anti-Inflammatory Foods: What to Eat (and Avoid) for Long-Term Health

Learn which foods drive chronic inflammation and which reduce it, with evidence-based guidance on building an anti-inflammatory diet you can actually maintain.

Forward Family Medicine April 8, 2025
dietary fiber health benefitsfiber and gut health

Why Fiber Is the Single Most Important Nutrient You're Probably Not Getting Enough Of

The Lancet's 2019 analysis found fiber intake is the dietary factor most consistently linked to better health outcomes. Most Americans get less than half the recommended amount.

Forward Family Medicine April 1, 2025
annual physical examwhat happens at annual physical

What to Expect at Your Annual Physical Exam (And Why It Matters)

A complete walkthrough of what happens at a thorough annual physical — beyond the basic wellness visit — and how to prepare to get the most from it.

Forward Family Medicine March 25, 2025
is preventive care worth itcost of preventive care

Is Preventive Care Actually Worth It? What the Research Shows

Not all preventive care is equally effective. Here is an honest, evidence-based look at what the research actually shows — and where preventive medicine delivers real value.

Forward Family Medicine March 18, 2025
doctor patient relationshipimportance of primary care relationship

Why the Doctor-Patient Relationship Is the Foundation of Good Health

Research shows that continuity of care with one physician reduces hospitalizations and improves outcomes. Here's what's lost when no one knows your whole story.

Forward Family Medicine March 11, 2025
patient-centered medical homePCMH vs DPC

What Is a Patient-Centered Medical Home (And Is It Different from DPC)?

PCMH and DPC both prioritize whole-person, coordinated care — but they differ fundamentally in how they handle insurance billing. Here's what that means for you.

Forward Family Medicine March 4, 2025
proactive medicinereactive healthcare

Proactive vs. Reactive Medicine: Why Most Healthcare Waits for You to Get Sick

The traditional healthcare system is built to respond to illness, not prevent it. Here's why, and what proactive medicine actually looks like in practice.

Forward Family Medicine February 25, 2025
does direct primary care save moneyDPC cost comparison

How Direct Primary Care Saves You Money: A Real Cost Comparison

DPC vs. traditional insurance: a real cost comparison including premiums, copays, lab pricing, and urgent care. For many patients, DPC + HDHP wins significantly.

Forward Family Medicine February 18, 2025
doctor panel sizepatient panel size primary care

Why Your Doctor's Patient Panel Size Affects Your Care

Traditional doctors manage 2,000–3,000 patients each. DPC doctors cap at 400–600. That difference in panel size explains almost everything about why primary care feels broken.

Forward Family Medicine February 11, 2025
direct primary care vs insuranceDPC vs traditional primary care

Direct Primary Care vs. Traditional Insurance: What's the Difference?

DPC and traditional insurance aren't competitors — they're different tools. Here's how they compare on cost, access, and visit quality, and how to use both wisely.

Forward Family Medicine February 4, 2025
concierge medicine costhow much does concierge medicine cost

How Much Does Concierge Medicine Cost? A Realistic Breakdown

Concierge medicine costs range from $75/month for DPC to $500+/month for traditional models. Here's what you actually get and whether it's worth it.

Forward Family Medicine January 28, 2025
DPC vs concierge medicineshould I choose DPC or concierge

DPC vs. Concierge Medicine: Which Is Right for You?

DPC and concierge medicine both offer better access and longer visits, but they differ in cost, insurance use, and scope. Here's how to choose.

Forward Family Medicine January 21, 2025
concierge medicine vs direct primary careDPC vs concierge

Concierge Medicine vs. Direct Primary Care: What's the Real Difference?

Concierge medicine and DPC both offer better access to your doctor, but the cost and billing structures are very different. Here's a clear comparison.

Forward Family Medicine January 14, 2025
what is concierge medicineconcierge medicine explained

What Is Concierge Medicine? A Patient's Complete Guide

Concierge medicine offers enhanced access, longer visits, and a direct physician relationship. Learn how it works, what it costs, and who it's best for.

Forward Family Medicine January 6, 2025

Our physicians write about what actually matters for your health — without the noise. New articles monthly, authored by Dr. Bigley or Dr. Lieberman.

© 2026 Forward Family Medicine. All rights reserved.

Forward Family Medicine is a Direct Primary Care practice. Membership is not insurance. We recommend pairing DPC with a high-deductible or health-sharing plan.
